records.ety: [L. praecedens, -entis, p. pr. of praecedere: cf. F. prcdent. See Precede.]

defs: Going before; anterior; preceding; antecedent; as, precedent services.

usage: -- Precedent, Example. An example in a similar case which may serve as a rule or guide, but has no authority out of itself. A precedent is something which comes down to us from the past with the sanction of usage and of common consent. We quote examples in literature, and precedents in law.

defs: Something done or said that may serve as an example to authorize a subsequent act of the same kind; an authoritative example.
quotes: Examples for cases can but direct as precedents only.

defs: A preceding circumstance or condition; an antecedent; hence, a prognostic; a token; a sign.
A rough draught of a writing which precedes a finished copy.

A judicial decision which serves as a rule for future determinations in similar or analogous cases; an authority to be followed in courts of justice; forms of proceeding to be followed in similar cases.
